info.name: Gym Management System - API
info.description: Endpoint collection derived from the team's microservices architecture and DB/ERD docs. All requests go through the API Gateway unless noted in the Internal folder. This is a starter scaffold - descriptions on each request flag what's confirmed vs. still open, to be filled in as each service gets built.

request.description:
Creates a new user in USERS (Auth Service, Postgres). Fields match the ERD: email, password_hash (hashed server-side, not the raw password), role, phone.

TO BE ADDED LATER: password strength rules, whether email verification is required before login, exact validation error format, and how 'role' gets set (self-selected at signup vs. assigned by admin for trainer accounts).

Validates credentials against USERS and returns a JWT for the client to use as {{token}} on all subsequent client-facing requests.

TO BE ADDED LATER: token expiry duration, response shape (does it return the user object alongside the token?), and lockout behavior after repeated failed attempts.

Returns the authenticated user's own USERS record based on the JWT in the Authorization header.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this also merges in profile data from User Profile Service, or stays Auth-only and the frontend calls both separately.

Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token, so the client doesn't have to re-login when the JWT expires.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whole refresh-token flow is undecided - is it a separate long-lived token issued at login, a cookie, or is this endpoint even in scope for phase 1?

Returns a CUSTOMER_PROFILES record by id (Postgres, User Profile Service).

TO BE ADDED LATER: confirm whether {{customer_id}} here is the profile id or the user_id FK - the ERD has both, and callers need to know which one this path expects.

Updates a customer's own profile fields (full_name, phone, date_of_birth, gender, photo_url per CUSTOMER_PROFILES).

TO BE ADDED LATER: how photo_url gets populated - is the image uploaded to S3/MinIO via this same request, or is there a separate upload endpoint that returns a URL to pass in here?

Returns a TRAINER_PROFILES record by id, including bio and specialty.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this response also embeds the trainer's CERTIFICATIONS list, or if that needs a separate call.

Updates a trainer's own profile fields (full_name, bio, specialty, gender, photo_url).

TO BE ADDED LATER: same file-upload question as the customer profile update - direct S3 upload here vs. separate endpoint.

body.raw:
{
  "full_name": "Coach Ahmed",
  "bio": "5 years of strength & conditioning coaching",
  "specialty": "Strength Training",
  "gender": "male",
  "photo_url": "https://cdn.example.com/photos/trainer1.jpg"
}

request.description:
Adds a CERTIFICATIONS row for a trainer (title, issuer, file_url, issued_date).

TO BE ADDED LATER: who's allowed to call this - trainer self-service, admin-only, or both? Also whether uploaded certs need admin approval before showing publicly on the trainer's profile.

Returns all PLANS (name, sessions/freeze/inbody included, duration, price). Public-ish catalog data, likely doesn't need auth to browse.

TO BE ADDED LATER: pagination, and whether inactive/retired plans are filtered out or included with a flag.

Admin-only: creates a new PLANS template.

TO BE ADDED LATER: admin-role enforcement details (how the Gateway/middleware knows this caller is an admin), and whether editing an existing plan is a separate PUT endpoint not yet listed here.

Creates a MEMBERSHIPS row, copying pt_sessions_left/freeze_days_left/inbody_left from the plan's included values at signup time (frozen snapshot, per the docs - later plan edits shouldn't retroactively change this customer's counts).

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this endpoint also requires proof of payment first, or if it's called after Payment Service confirms via the PaymentSucceeded event - this is one of the saga flow's open questions.

Returns the customer's current MEMBERSHIPS row - status, dates, and remaining benefit counts.

TO BE ADDED LATER: behavior if a customer has no active membership (404 vs. empty object), and whether membership history (past/expired ones) is available here or needs a separate endpoint.

Deducts from freeze_days_left and presumably extends end_date accordingly.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whole freeze mechanic is still open - does it take effect immediately or does the customer pick future start/end dates for the freeze? Not detailed in the architecture docs yet.

Returns CLASSES (name, type, capacity) - the catalog of class types, not specific time slots.

TO BE ADDED LATER: filtering by type, and whether this should be combined with CLASS_SESSIONS in one call so the frontend gets both the class and its upcoming times together.

Returns TRAINER_SLOTS filtered by trainer and date - the open time windows a customer can book a PT session into.

TO BE ADDED LATER: exact query param set (date range vs. single date), and whether already-booked slots are excluded server-side or returned with a status field for the frontend to filter.

Creates a BOOKINGS row as pending, after an internal sync check against Membership Service for available sessions (see Internal folder). Per the saga flow, this likely starts in a pending_payment-style status and only becomes confirmed once PaymentSucceeded fires.

TO BE ADDED LATER: exact status values this can start/end in, and what the response looks like while payment is still pending - the client needs to know how to react (poll? websocket? notification?).

Cancels a booking and presumably releases the trainer slot back to open status.

TO BE ADDED LATER: cancellation policy (cutoff window, whether PT sessions get refunded to the membership balance), and whether this also needs to trigger a compensating event if payment was already taken.

Moves a booking to a different trainer slot.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this is atomic (old slot released + new slot claimed in one step) and how the status = 'open' race-condition check applies to the new slot, same as initial booking creation.

Charges the customer via the payment gateway (Paymob/Stripe-style provider) - this call itself must be synchronous per the architecture doc, not eventually consistent. reference_type/reference_id link back to whatever's being paid for (booking, order, or membership).

TO BE ADDED LATER: which provider is actually being integrated, the exact success/failure response shape from that provider, and how retries are handled if the gateway times out.

Returns a single PAYMENTS record with its PAYMENT_ITEMS.

TO BE ADDED LATER: nothing major open here - fairly standard read, will confirm once implemented.

Returns all payments made by a customer.

TO BE ADDED LATER: pagination and date-range filtering, and whether failed/pending payments show up here alongside successful ones.

Returns PRODUCTS catalog (name, category, price, stock_qty).

TO BE ADDED LATER: filtering/search params, and whether out-of-stock products are hidden or shown as disabled.

Creates an ORDERS row plus ORDER_ITEMS, and per the architecture doc should reserve stock at this point (not just decrement it later).

TO BE ADDED LATER: how stock reservation is implemented (a hold/timeout mechanism if payment never completes), and confirm the payment step happens sync or via event after this call, matching Flow C in the architecture doc.

Returns an ORDERS record with its items.

TO BE ADDED LATER: nothing major - standard read, will confirm response shape once implemented.

Admin-only: adjusts stock_qty directly.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this is a set-to-value or an increment/decrement operation, and admin-role enforcement details.

Creates a PROGRESS_ENTRIES document (MongoDB) - weight, body fat %, InBody data, nutrition log for a given date.

TO BE ADDED LATER: exact shape of inbody_data and nutrition_log objects (not detailed in the ERD beyond 'object'), and whether this publishes ProgressUpdated for AI/trainer review as mentioned in the architecture doc.

Returns a customer's progress entries over time.

TO BE ADDED LATER: date range filtering, and sort order (likely newest-first, needs confirming).

Creates a TRAINER_PLANS document. created_by can be 'trainer', 'ai', or 'admin' per the ERD, and status starts somewhere in the pending_customer_review -> pending_trainer_review -> active/rejected chain depending on who created it.

TO BE ADDED LATER: exact shape of workout_days and nutrition_targets objects, and which status this starts at for each created_by value (a trainer-created plan probably skips straight to active, an AI-created one starts at pending_customer_review).

Moves a plan through its status chain (e.g. customer approves -> pending_trainer_review, trainer accepts -> active).

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether each transition is its own endpoint (approve/reject) instead of one generic status PUT - worth checking with whoever builds this, a generic status field is easy to misuse from the frontend.

Returns a customer's trainer plans.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this returns all plans regardless of status, or only active ones by default with a query param to include others.

Returns community THREADS.

TO BE ADDED LATER: pagination, sort order, and whether reply counts are included inline or require a separate call.

Creates a new THREADS document.

TO BE ADDED LATER: moderation rules (can admin delete/hide threads?), and whether trainers vs customers see the same community space or separate ones.

Creates a THREAD_REPLIES document linked to a thread.

TO BE ADDED LATER: nested replies (reply to a reply) or flat only - the ERD only shows a flat thread_id FK, worth confirming that's intentional.

Creates a DIRECT_MESSAGES document. Also delivered over WebSocket per the architecture doc, so REST here is likely just for sending/history, not the real-time delivery path.

TO BE ADDED LATER: how the WebSocket and REST paths relate - does this REST call also push over the socket, or does the frontend handle that separately?

Returns direct message history between two users. The docs note this needs an index on sender_id+receiver_id (or a computed conversation_id) to be efficient.

TO BE ADDED LATER: pagination for long conversation histories, and confirm the URL shape (this assumes /messages/{userA}/{userB} but a query-param version is equally valid).

Increments the live occupancy counter in Redis. Per the docs this may come from a check-in device rather than the app itself, over REST or MQTT.

TO BE ADDED LATER: is this actually called by the customer's app, a physical gate device, or both? Changes who the caller/auth model is for this endpoint.

Decrements the live occupancy counter.

TO BE ADDED LATER: same open question as check-in regarding caller/device, plus what happens if a checkout never arrives (auto-expire after N hours?).

Returns the current people-in-gym count. Per the docs the live version of this is pushed via WebSocket/pub-sub - this REST version is likely just a one-off snapshot read.

TO BE ADDED LATER: confirm this REST endpoint is actually needed alongside the WebSocket stream, or if it's redundant.

Returns published ANNOUNCEMENTS.

TO BE ADDED LATER: filtering by category, and whether unpublished/scheduled announcements are visible to admins here or through a separate admin-only endpoint.

Admin-only: creates an ANNOUNCEMENTS document, which should trigger AnnouncementCreated for Notification Service.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether published_at can be set in the future for scheduled announcements, or if this always publishes immediately.

Returns AI_PLANS - tiered subscription options with feature flags (video_analysis, custom_nutrition, etc).

TO BE ADDED LATER: which database this service actually uses - the architecture doc says MongoDB, the ERD shows Postgres with AI_PLANS/AI_SUBSCRIPTIONS/AI_USAGE_LOG. This is a real inconsistency between the two docs your team should resolve before building against it.

Creates an AI_SUBSCRIPTIONS row, copying interactions_left from the plan's credits_number (same frozen-snapshot pattern as Membership).

TO BE ADDED LATER: same DB-choice question as above, plus whether payment is required before this call, matching the same open question as Membership subscription.

Kicks off Flow B from the architecture doc: AI Service checks Booking Service internally for real trainer availability, then proposes a schedule back to the customer.

TO BE ADDED LATER: response shape while the AI is 'thinking' (sync wait vs. async job the client polls), and how the proposed schedule maps to TRAINER_PLANS status once the customer approves it.

General AI chatbot interaction, deducts from interactions_left and logs to AI_USAGE_LOG.

TO BE ADDED LATER: which external LLM API this calls, streaming vs. single response, and what happens when interactions_left hits zero.

Returns a user's notifications. Notification Service listens to nearly every event in the system per the architecture doc, so this is likely the largest fan-in consumer.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this service is stateless (per the docs, 'MongoDB or none') - if notifications aren't persisted, this endpoint may not exist at all and delivery is push-only. Needs a decision before documenting further.

Marks a notification as read.

TO BE ADDED LATER: same open question as above - depends on whether notifications are persisted at all.

item.name: Internal - Service to Service
item.description:
NOT reached through the API Gateway. These are direct service-to-service calls used inside sync flows described in the architecture doc (e.g. Booking checking Membership before confirming a paid session). Auth here is a service token (service_token variable), not a user JWT - do not reuse {{token}} here. Base URLs point at each service directly, not the gateway. Placeholder scaffold - fill in real paths/bodies once these are actually implemented.

TO BE ADDED LATER (team-wide, still open as of now): whether internal auth ends up being a shared-secret token or something mesh-based - decision depends on whether EKS is a confirmed deployment target.

request.description:
Called by Booking Service before confirming a PT session booking. Answers: does this customer have PT sessions left on their membership right now? Sync call - Booking cannot proceed without this answer.

TO BE ADDED LATER: exact response shape (just a boolean, or the full remaining-benefits object), and timeout/fallback behavior if Membership Service doesn't respond in time.

item.name: AI Service -> Booking: Check Trainer/Slot Availability

request.description:
Called by AI Service while generating a proposed schedule. Answers: is this trainer free at this time? Sync call - AI Service needs a real answer before it can propose a schedule to the customer.

TO BE ADDED LATER: whether this checks a single time slot per call or accepts a batch of candidate times to reduce round trips during schedule generation.
